data.text/html:
<style>/* Load fonts from Google Fonts */
@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:opsz,wght@14..32,300..800&family=Fira+Code:wght@300..700&display=swap");

/* Font and Radius Variables */
:root {
    --monospace-font: "Fira Code", monospace;
    --text-font: "Inter", system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", "Roboto", "Open Sans", sans-serif;
    --heading-font: "Inter", system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", "Roboto", "Open Sans", sans-serif;
    --radius: 12px;
}

/* Mininini Theme Colors */
:root {
    /* Backgrounds - soft, not pure black */
    --background: light-dark(#ffffff, #181a1c);
    --background-darker: light-dark(#e5e5e5, #101214);
    --foreground: light-dark(#0a0a0a, #eef2f5);
    --muted: light-dark(#f0f0f0, #222528);
    --muted-foreground: light-dark(#777777, #9aa3ae);

    /* UI Elements */
    --card: light-dark(#fafafa, #1e2124);
    --card-foreground: light-dark(#222222, #eef2f5);
    --popover: light-dark(#e8e8e8, #181a1c);
    --popover-foreground: light-dark(#0f0f0f, #eef2f5);
    --border: light-dark(#444444, #2e3236);
    --input: light-dark(#f5f5f5, #131517);

    /* Primary Colors */
    --primary: light-dark(#1e40af, #3b82f6);
    --primary-foreground: light-dark(#ffffff, #ffffff);
    --secondary: light-dark(#475569, #6c7a91);
    --secondary-foreground: light-dark(#ffffff, #f1f5f9);
    --accent: light-dark(#f97316, #fb923c);
    --accent-foreground: light-dark(#ffffff, #ffffff);

    /* Heading Colors - All gray except H4 (orange) and H5 (blue) */
    --h1-color: light-dark(#1f2937, #e5e7eb); /* Gray */
    --h2-color: light-dark(#374151, #d1d5db); /* Gray */
    --h3-color: light-dark(#4b5563, #9ca3af); /* Gray */
    --h4-color: light-dark(#ea580c, #f97316); /* Orange accent */
    --h5-color: light-dark(#2563eb, #60a5fa); /* Blue accent */
    --h6-color: light-dark(#6b7280, #9ca3af); /* Gray (lighter) */

    /* Code & Math Styling */
    --inline-code-color: light-dark(#2563eb, #60a5fa);
    --equation-color: light-dark(#6b7280, #9ca3af);

    /* Semantic Colors */
    --error: light-dark(#dc2626, #f87171);
    --success: light-dark(#059669, #34d399);
    --destructive: light-dark(#b91c1c, #ef4444);
    --action: light-dark(#d97706, #fbbf24);
    --link: light-dark(#2563eb, #3b82f6);
    --link-visited: light-dark(#7c3aed, #a78bfa);

    /* Shadows */
    --base-shadow: light-dark(rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08), rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3));
    --base-shadow-darker: light-dark(rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12), rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4));
    --base-shadow-opacity: 8%;
}

/* Heading styles */
h1, .h1 { 
    color: var(--h1-color); 
    font-weight: 800;
    letter-spacing: -0.02em;
}

h2, .h2 { 
    color: var(--h2-color); 
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: -0.01em;
}

h3, .h3 { 
    color: var(--h3-color); 
    font-weight: 600;
}

h4, .h4 { 
    color: var(--h4-color); /* Orange */
    font-weight: 600; 
    letter-spacing: -0.01em; 
}

h5, .h5 { 
    color: var(--h5-color); /* Blue */
    font-weight: 500;
}

h6, .h6 { 
    color: var(--h6-color); /* Gray */
    font-weight: 500;
}

/* Inline code styling - Blue */
code:not(pre code), .inline-code {
    color: var(--inline-code-color);
    font-family: var(--monospace-font);
    background-color: var(--muted);
    padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-size: 0.9em;
    font-weight: 500;
}

/* Equation styling - Gray */
marimo-tex,
.arithmatex,
.marimo-tex,
.katex,
.katex *,
.mjx-container,
mjx-container,
.math {
    color: var(--equation-color) !important;
}

marimo-tex mjx-container,
marimo-tex .MathJax,
marimo-tex [class*="math"] {
    color: var(--equation-color) !important;
}

.latex,
.latex *,
.tex,
.tex * {
    color: var(--equation-color) !important;
}

/* Code blocks subtle blue tint */
pre code {
    background-color: var(--background-darker);
    border-left: 3px solid var(--primary);
}

.markdown marimo-tex,
.prose marimo-tex {
    color: var(--equation-color) !important;
}
</style>

data.text/markdown:
Dynamic Programming is an algorithmic technique with the following properties.
  1. It is mainly an optimization over plain recursion. Wherever we see a recursive solution that has repeated calls for the same inputs, we can optimize it using Dynamic Programming.
  2. The idea is to simply store the results of subproblems so that we do not have to re-compute them when needed later.
Some popular problems solved using Dynamic Programming are Fibonacci Numbers, Diff Utility (Longest Common Subsequence), Bellman–Ford Shortest Path, Floyd Warshall, Edit Distance and Matrix Chain Multiplication.
Staircase Problem
